name: Deng Ai
description: Deng Ai (197–264), courtesy name Shizai, was a military general of the state of Cao Wei during the Three Kingdoms period of China. He is best known for his surprise attack on Shu Han in 263, which led to the fall of Shu. Deng Ai initially served under Sima Yi, the regent of Wei, and distinguished himself through his strategic planning and logistical expertise. He proposed and implemented innovative agricultural policies to ensure the Wei army's self-sufficiency during long campaigns. His most famous achievement was his daring march through treacherous mountain terrain, bypassing Shu's heavily defended passes. This unexpected maneuver caught the Shu forces off guard, leading to the capture of Jiang Wei and ultimately the surrender of the Shu emperor Liu Shan. Despite his success, Deng Ai's arrogance and independent actions aroused suspicion from Sima Zhao, Sima Yi's successor. He was falsely accused of treason and arrested, leading to his death at the hands of Tian Xu, a general who resented Deng Ai. Deng Ai's life exemplifies both military genius and the dangers of political intrigue. He remains a controversial figure, admired for his strategic brilliance but also criticized for his ambition and eventual downfall. He is often depicted in Chinese opera and folklore as a complex and tragic hero.
subtitle: The General Who Conquered Shu
achievements: Successful defense against Jiang Wei's invasions

keyMyths.title: The Surprise Attack on Shu
keyMyths.description: Deng Ai led his troops through seemingly impassable mountains, surprising the Shu forces and leading to their downfall. This daring maneuver is his most famous achievement. He circumvented established defenses to achieve victory.

keyMyths.title: The Agricultural Reforms
keyMyths.description: Deng Ai implemented agricultural policies to ensure the Wei army's self-sufficiency during long campaigns. This involved establishing farms and irrigation systems in conquered territories. These policies were critical for sustaining the war effort.

keyMyths.title: Deng Ai's Downfall
keyMyths.description: Deng Ai's success and independent actions aroused suspicion from Sima Zhao, leading to false accusations of treason. He was arrested and subsequently killed by Tian Xu. Political intrigue was his undoing.

sources.author: Chen Shou
sources.description: The primary historical source for Deng Ai's life and deeds.
sources.text: The primary historical source for Deng Ai's life and deeds.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A fictionalized account of Deng Ai's life, portraying him as a complex and often tragic figure.
